title: pinus sylvestris subsp. montana
intro: Pinus sylvestris subsp. montana, also known as mountain Scots pine, is a subspecies of the Scots pine tree (Pinus sylvestris) native to mountainous regions of Europe. This coniferous evergreen has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of mountain Scots pine include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Pinus sylvestris subsp. montana include essential oils, tea, and infusions, which can be used to promote relaxation and alleviate respiratory issues.
